title: Best Password Manager for Small Business and Teams (2026)
excerpt: Shared credentials are one of the top attack vectors in small business breaches. Here's how the leading team password managers compare in 2026 — covering price per seat, admin controls, and SSO support.
content:
Why Teams Need a Dedicated Password Manager

Consumer password managers solve your personal credential problem. Business password managers solve a harder one: how do you share credentials safely, control who has access to what, offboard ex-employees instantly, and prove to auditors that your team follows password hygiene? Studies consistently show that compromised or reused credentials are the entry point in over 80% of data breaches. For small businesses handling customer data, a single shared "company admin" password represents catastrophic risk.

The right team password manager gives you: centralized vault administration, role-based access control, audit logs, and one-click offboarding that revokes access across every shared credential simultaneously.

Top Business Password Managers Compared (2026)

ProductPrice/seat/moMin seatsSSO/SCIMAdmin console
1Password Teams$41Business tierExcellent
Bitwarden Teams$41Enterprise tierGood
NordPass Business$4.991✅ IncludedGood
Dashlane Business$81✅ IncludedExcellent

1Password Teams — Best All-Around

1Password Teams at $4/seat/month is the best combination of usability and admin capability for most small businesses. The admin console gives you fine-grained vault sharing (you control exactly which employees see which credential groups), detailed activity logs, and two-factor enforcement policy. SSO and SCIM provisioning are available on the Business tier ($8/seat/month) — relevant once you're using Okta, Azure AD, or Google Workspace for identity. For teams under 20 people where SSO isn't a requirement, Teams is the sweet spot.

Bitwarden Teams — Best Value

Bitwarden Teams at $4/seat/month is the open-source alternative. You get shared organization vaults, user groups, event logs, and directory connector for SCIM provisioning. The admin experience isn't as polished as 1Password, but the open-source architecture means your security team can audit exactly how encryption works. For technical teams or companies with a strict open-source policy, Bitwarden is the obvious choice.

NordPass Business — Best for SSO Without Enterprise Pricing

NordPass Business includes SSO at its base $4.99/seat/month price — a significant advantage over 1Password and Bitwarden, which reserve SSO for higher tiers. If your team already uses a SAML identity provider and you want to enforce SSO-only login, NordPass Business is unusually cost-effective. It uses XChaCha20 encryption (an alternative to AES-256) which some security teams prefer for its modern design.

Dashlane Business — Best Admin Features

Dashlane at $8/seat/month is the premium option with the most comprehensive admin dashboard, including real-time breach monitoring per employee, a security health score across the entire organization, and built-in VPN. For businesses handling sensitive client data or operating in regulated industries, the audit and compliance tooling is worth the premium.

Key Features to Look For

Immediate offboarding: When an employee leaves, you need to revoke their access to shared credentials in seconds, not days. Every platform here supports instant offboarding from the admin console.

Audit logs: Who accessed what credential, and when? Audit logs are essential for SOC 2 and GDPR compliance, and for incident response if you suspect a breach.

Zero-knowledge architecture: Your password manager provider should not be able to read your vault. All four options above use client-side encryption — your keys never hit their servers.

Recommendation

For most small businesses (5–50 people), start with 1Password Teams. If you need SSO from day one and want to keep costs down, NordPass Business is the smarter pick. Make sure every team member uses our free password generator when creating new credentials, and read our full security tools guide for additional recommendations.
